Finance & Economy.headline: S&P affirms UAE sovereign credit ratings at AA/A-1+ amid regional tensions
Finance & Economy.detail: S&P affirmed the UAE's sovereign credit ratings, indicating stability despite geopolitical tensions.

Finance & Economy.headline: Loblaw investigates data breach after identifying suspicious activity
Finance & Economy.detail: Loblaw is currently investigating a potential data breach following the identification of suspicious activities within its systems.

Finance & Economy.headline: Ottawa drops TikTok ban, will now let platform stay in Canada with conditions
Finance & Economy.detail: The Canadian government has decided to lift the ban on TikTok, allowing the platform to operate in Canada under specific conditions.

Finance & Economy.headline: Aramco CEO sees 'catastrophic consequences' for oil if shipping doesn't resume in Strait of Hormuz
Finance & Economy.detail: The CEO of Aramco warns of potential catastrophic consequences for the oil industry if shipping operations are not restored in the Strait of Hormuz.

Finance & Economy.headline: Dassault pitches latest private jet against Canadian, U.S. rivals
Finance & Economy.detail: Dassault Aviation is promoting its newest private jet model to compete with rivals from Canada and the United States.

Technology.headline: ChatGPT, other AI chatbots approved for official use in US Senate, NYT reports
Technology.detail: ChatGPT, Gemini, and Microsoft Copilot have been approved for official use in the U.S. Senate, according to a New York Times report, with aides now able to use these AI chatbots which are already integrated into Senate platforms. A Microsoft spokesperson stated that the company is looking into the approval, while Google and OpenAI have not yet responded to requests for comment.

Technology.headline: AI semiconductor market shifts focus from HBM to low-power DRAM module Socamm2
Technology.detail: Competition in the AI semiconductor market is expanding beyond high bandwidth memory (HBM) to server low-power dynamic random-access memory (DRAM) modules known as Socamm2. Micron has shipped the world's first customer samples of a 256GB Socamm2 module, which is about 33% larger than the 192GB Socamm2 products from Samsung Electronics and SK hynix.

Technology.headline: Cyber partnership strengthens across Tasman
Technology.detail: Australia and New Zealand are strengthening their cyber partnership, with New Zealand gaining access to Australia's Persistent Cyber Training Environment – Unclassified (PCTE-U), a platform developed by the U.S. Department of Defense. This agreement enhances collective training and preparedness, ensuring both countries can confront cyber challenges and defend shared security interests in the digital battlespace.

Technology.headline: Lotus Tech Launches Pre-Sales for LOTUS For Me in China
Technology.detail: Lotus Technology Inc. has commenced pre-sales for LOTUS For Me, its hyper-SUV, in the Chinese market, with pre-sale prices starting at Rmb528,000 (approximately USD $76,800). The entire lineup will be built on Geely Afari Smart Driving (G-ASD) H7 architecture and will come standard with the NVIDIA Thor flagship computing chip, enabling highway and urban Navigation on Autopilot (NOA) functionalities.

Technology.headline: K-12 IT leader flags YouTube workaround in Google tools
Technology.detail: A technology director in a New York school district has raised concerns about a workaround in Google Slides that allows students to bypass YouTube restrictions, prompting responses from Google for Education and other K-12 technology leaders. Students can paste a YouTube link directly into a Google Slides presentation and click through for a full-screen preview, bypassing content filters.

Science.headline: Young magpies learn complex 'sentences' just like humans
Science.detail: New research indicates that young magpies learn to transform basic sounds into complex calls and sequences, akin to how human children develop language skills, providing the first evidence of learned syntax in a non-human animal. Social connection was found to be a key factor in how quickly and effectively individual fledglings learned.

Science.headline: Microsoft shifts AI research focus as Peter Lee takes on President of Microsoft Science role
Science.detail: Peter Lee's new role as President of Microsoft Science will focus on accelerating scientific discovery using AI, as Microsoft expands its work at the intersection of artificial intelligence and scientific research. Lee says that AI-driven transformation is likely to extend into multiple research domains, and the most important frontier has to do with how we do research in the physical, biological, and medical sciences.

Science.headline: Regeneron Science Talent Search 2026 Recognizes America's Top Young Scientists, Awarding More Than $1.8 Million to High School Seniors for Innovative Research in Computational Mathematics, Neural Science, and Blood Cancer Treatment
Science.detail: Connor Hill, a 17-year-old from Pennsylvania, won the top award of $250,000 in the 2026 Regeneron Science Talent Search for discovering a way to identify all the possible "noble polyhedra". Edward Kang, 17, of Hackensack, New Jersey won second place for using retinal images to train AI models on subtle patterns linked to autism and attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder to create a screening tool called RetinaMind.

Science.headline: Nuclear energy central to Rwanda's dev't strategy: president
Science.detail: Rwandan President Paul Kagame stated that nuclear energy is central to Rwanda's ambitions under Vision 2050 and called for international cooperation in financing and technology. He noted that nuclear technology is evolving to benefit countries with small grids, making Africa an early adopter of small modular reactors (SMRs).

Science.headline: Why anger feels close to fear: Brain charts emotion in a map-like way.
Science.detail: Researchers have found that anger feels close to fear because the brain charts emotion in a map-like way. This study highlights how the brain organizes and processes different emotions.

Health.headline: Daily multivitamin delays biological aging by up to 5 months, trial…
Health.detail: A recent trial suggests that taking a daily multivitamin may delay biological aging by up to 5 months.

Health.headline: Extended Dupilumab Dosing Maintains Efficacy in Nasal Polyps
Health.detail: A recent study indicates that extended dosing of Dupilumab continues to show effectiveness in treating nasal polyps.

Health.headline: Statins After 80? Lower Death Risk With Heart Benefit
Health.detail: Research suggests that statin use in individuals over 80 years old is associated with a reduced risk of death and provides heart benefits.

Health.headline: Stanford Scientists Say Colorblindness May Hide a Deadly Bladder Cancer Warning
Health.detail: Researchers analyzing millions of medical records found that people with colorblindness may have a hidden warning sign for bladder cancer.

Health.headline: Researchers Find Alarming Alzheimer's Pattern in Rural America
Health.detail: A new analysis reveals a significant Alzheimer's care disparity between urban and rural communities in Maryland, with rural regions lacking essential resources.

Entertainment.headline: Woman charged with attempted murder after weekend shooting at Rihanna's L.A. home
Entertainment.detail: A Florida woman accused of firing gunshots at Rihanna's home in Los Angeles was charged Tuesday with one count of attempted murder and other felony offences, including 10 counts of assault on a person with a semi-automatic firearm.

Entertainment.headline: Long lost George Michael film and live album set for release later this year
Entertainment.detail: A previously unreleased George Michael film and live album are scheduled for release later in 2026, offering fans a glimpse into the late singer's performances.

Entertainment.headline: Boston frontman Tommy DeCarlo dies aged 60
Entertainment.detail: Tommy DeCarlo, the frontman for the band Boston, has passed away at the age of 60.

Entertainment.headline: Tarantino hits back at Rosanna Arquette's criticism of N-word use in his films
Entertainment.detail: Quentin Tarantino has responded to Rosanna Arquette's criticism regarding his use of the N-word in his films.

Entertainment.headline: The Heated Rivalry Cast Doesn't Want 'Bigoted' Fans
Entertainment.detail: Hudson Williams and François Arnaud, stars of 'Heated Rivalry,' released a statement, also shared by several co-stars, condemning hateful comments and stating that they don't need hateful 'love'.

Crime & Justice.headline: Two ISIS Supporters Charged with Attempting to Detonate Explosive Devices During Protests Outside Gracie Mansion
Crime & Justice.detail: Two individuals who allegedly support ISIS have been charged after attempting to detonate explosive devices during protests outside of New York City Mayor's residence. The suspects face federal charges after allegedly trying to detonate homemade bombs, inspired by ISIS, near Mayor Zohran Mamdani's home.
Crime & Justice.source: CBS News / U.S. Department of Justice

Crime & Justice.headline: Alexander brothers found guilty at trial
Crime & Justice.detail: After a trial, the Alexander brothers were found guilty on all counts of sex trafficking after being arrested more than a year ago. The jury heard from 11 women who accused the brothers of rape or sexual assault.

Crime & Justice.headline: US teacher killed in toilet paper prank gone wrong, teen charged
Crime & Justice.detail: A 40-year-old teacher died after a group of teenagers TP'd his house, and as they were driving away, the teacher tripped in the street and was struck by their pickup truck. The 18-year-old driver was arrested on a felony charge of vehicular homicide, and four other teens were charged with misdemeanors.

Crime & Justice.headline: Department of Justice Releases First-Ever Corporate Enforcement Policy for All Criminal Cases
Crime & Justice.detail: The Department of Justice has released its first-ever Corporate Enforcement Policy applicable to all criminal cases. This policy likely outlines guidelines and priorities for prosecuting corporations involved in criminal activities.

Crime & Justice.headline: Mexican National Pleads Guilty to Racketeering Conspiracy Involving the Forced Labor of Mexican Workers
Crime & Justice.detail: A Mexican national has pleaded guilty to racketeering conspiracy for their involvement in the forced labor of Mexican workers. The details of the case likely involve exploitation and coercion within a criminal enterprise.

Education.headline: Education Ministry Announces Additional School Holiday On 18 March
Education.detail: The Education Ministry in Malaysia has declared an additional school holiday on March 18th, effectively extending the Hari Raya school break to nine days for students nationwide.

Education.headline: School Assembly News Headlines Today (March 11): Top National, Business News, Sports News, Education News & World News with Weather Updates & Thought of the Day
Education.detail: CHSE Odisha introduced an improvement exam option for Plus Two students, and the government is emphasizing linking education with the real economy to improve employability. Education policy discussions are also highlighting the need to strengthen innovation and skill-based learning in universities.

Education.headline: Nervous kids on standby as NAPLAN testing put on hold
Education.detail: NAPLAN testing in South Australia has been paused due to issues with the online platform, affecting over 1.3 million students and causing disappointment for many nervous students. The Education Minister stated that the technology provider is urgently investigating the issue and schools have been advised to pause testing.

Education.headline: New AITSL Guidelines recognise importance of teaching assistants
Education.detail: New guidelines from AITSL emphasize the crucial role teaching assistants play in enhancing student outcomes and reducing teacher workloads in Australian classrooms. The guidelines highlight the benefits of a school-wide approach to deploying teaching assistants, with clear role expectations, collaboration time, and professional learning opportunities.

Education.headline: Education Department urged to broaden 'professional' student definition
Education.detail: The Education Department is being urged to broaden the definition of a 'professional' student, as the agency's proposed rule would limit graduate students in education from being able to borrow no more than $100,000 in federal student loans. This could impact graduate students in education who may need additional funding for their studies.

Lifestyle.headline: Louis Vuitton, Miu Miu close Paris Fashion Week with competing visions of nature
Lifestyle.detail: Louis Vuitton's Nicolas Ghesquière built a fake mountain range inside the Louvre, showcasing folklore-inspired high fashion, while Miuccia Prada presented a contrasting view, emphasizing the individual's inherent sufficiency with minimalist clothing. Celebrities like Zendaya and Ana de Armas attended the Louis Vuitton show.

Lifestyle.headline: Five Disneyland employees hospitalized after backstage chemical incident near Star Tours causes foul odor
Lifestyle.detail: Five Disneyland employees were hospitalized following a chemical reaction in a backstage area near Star Tours that caused a foul odor. The incident is under investigation.

Lifestyle.headline: Harrogate Fundraiser to share story of 9,000-mile unsupported bike ride to the himalayas
Lifestyle.detail: A Harrogate resident is sharing their experience of completing a 9,000-mile unsupported bike ride to the Himalayas at a fundraiser. The event aims to inspire others and raise money for a good cause.

Lifestyle.headline: Boy, 4, left alone throws toys from window, smashes BMW, mother held
Lifestyle.detail: A four-year-old boy in Sham Shui Po, Hong Kong, was allegedly left home alone and threw toys out the window, damaging a parked BMW; his mother was subsequently arrested. The incident highlights concerns about child neglect and safety.

Lifestyle.headline: Americans unite in aggravation over sticker shock at the gas pump in a time of war with Iran
Lifestyle.detail: In a time of war with Iran, Americans are experiencing widespread frustration due to rising gasoline prices. This has become a unifying issue across the country.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Unlucky country: Lethargic leadership during crisis
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Marcos administration's response to the potential geopolitical shock and energy crisis appears inadequate, relying on hope rather than decisive policy and leadership. The administration's most visible policy response has been to tell government employees to conserve electricity while the president travels abroad, and for certain government agencies to be open only four days a week.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on Europe's response to the Iran crisis: damage limitation only goes so far
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses Europe's response to the ongoing Iran crisis. It emphasizes that damage limitation measures are insufficient to address the deeper issues.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Never again
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses the anniversary of former president Rodrigo Duterte's arrest and turnover to the International Criminal Court (ICC) for alleged crimes against humanity. It emphasizes the importance of ensuring that the abuses of his war on drugs are never repeated.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: The future relies on diverse energy
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial argues that Taiwan must enhance its energy resilience by diversifying its sources to avoid surges in imported fuel costs amidst the escalating US-Israeli war on Iran. It highlights Taiwan's heavy reliance on imported fuel and the need to beef up its energy transformation efforts.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL — Should PH also ban social media for people under 16?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses the potential benefits and drawbacks of banning social media for individuals under 16 years old in the Philippines. It cites the impact of social media on the mental health of young people, including short attention spans, increased anxiety and depression, and rising suicide rates.

World.headline: 6 people killed and 3 hospitalized in Switzerland bus fire, police say
World.detail: A bus fire in Switzerland has resulted in the death of six people and the hospitalization of three others, according to police reports.

World.headline: Australia grants asylum to 2 more members of Iranian women's soccer team
World.detail: Two more members of the Iranian women's soccer team have been granted asylum in Australia after seeking assistance.

World.headline: Iran says US, Israel have hit nearly 10,000 civilian sites since war began
World.detail: Iran claims that the US and Israel have struck almost 10,000 civilian locations since the beginning of the war.

World.headline: Air raid sirens blare in Bahrain
World.detail: Air raid sirens were activated in Bahrain.

World.headline: Drone hits U.S. diplomatic facility in Iraq, official says; no known casualties
World.detail: A U.S. diplomatic facility in Iraq was hit by a drone, with no casualties reported; the attack is suspected to be a retaliation by pro-Tehran militias against the U.S. and Israeli-led war on Iran.

Zimbabwe.headline: Rampant Panthers outclass Suns to stay top of Matabeleland North League
Zimbabwe.detail: Panthers displayed a dominant performance to maintain their lead in the Matabeleland North League.

Zimbabwe.headline: BATTLE OF ZIM SET TO SHIFT PSL GEAR
Zimbabwe.detail: The Premier Soccer League (PSL) is gearing up for a significant shift with the upcoming 'Battle of Zimbabwe' match.

Zimbabwe.headline: Where will the goals come from? . . Mangombe feels weight of big clash at Rufaro
Zimbabwe.detail: Coach Mangombe is feeling the pressure ahead of the big clash at Rufaro stadium, with questions surrounding the team's goal-scoring capabilities.

Zimbabwe.headline: DeMbare secure medical partnership deal
Zimbabwe.detail: DeMbare has entered into a medical partnership, ensuring better healthcare support for its players.

Zimbabwe.headline: Highlanders, Dynamos clash in headline fixture of Week 2
Zimbabwe.detail: The highly anticipated match between Highlanders and Dynamos is set to be the highlight of Week 2.
